title: ProvisioningResponse
description: HTTP 200 response body returned by the server's provisioning endpoint (POST /api/v1/stations/provision) per Chapter 04 — Protocol Flows §2. Contains the station's signed identity, trust anchors, server signing key, and the MQTT connection parameters required to bootstrap the operational mTLS session described in Chapter 02 — Transport §1.

stationId.description: Server-assigned station identifier. MUST equal the CN in the issued client certificate (Chapter 06 — Security §4.4).

bayIds.description: Server-assigned bay identifiers belonging to this station. Maximum bay count per controller is 255 (Chapter 01 — Architecture §4.2).

clientCert.description: PEM-encoded X.509 client certificate signed by the Station CA. ECDSA P-256, Subject CN = stationId, EKU clientAuth, validity ≤ 1 year (Chapter 06 — Security §4.4).

stationCaChain.description: PEM-encoded Station CA chain (Station CA, optionally followed by Root CA) used by the broker to verify the station's client certificate during mTLS. May contain multiple concatenated PEM blocks.

brokerRootCa.description: PEM-encoded trust anchor (Root CA, or Root + intermediate chain) used by the station to validate the broker's TLS server certificate during mTLS. OPTIONAL: when present, station MUST use as trust anchor; when absent, station MAY use system trust store. May contain multiple concatenated PEM blocks.

rootCaThumbprint.description: OPTIONAL. SHA-256 thumbprint of the DER-encoded Root CA certificate, formatted as `sha256:<64 lowercase hex>`. Enables a station to pin the Root CA trust anchor (e.g. in NVS) and confirm it matches the apex of stationCaChain. Stations that do not implement trust-anchor pinning MAY ignore this field; servers are not required to emit it.

serverVerifyKey.description: PEM-encoded ECDSA P-256 server signing public key used by the station to verify OfflinePass and ServerSignedAuth signatures (Chapter 06 — Security §4.2 §6.1 §6.7).

brokerHost.description: MQTT broker hostname (RFC 1035 FQDN) or IPv4 address. The station MUST be able to resolve this from its deployed network.

brokerPort.description: MQTT broker TCP port. Chapter 02 — Transport §1.4 mandates port 8883 for MQTT-over-TLS. Plaintext MQTT (1883) MUST NOT be used in any environment.

brokerUri.description: Convenience pre-formatted broker URI. The mqtts:// scheme is REQUIRED — plaintext mqtt:// MUST NOT be used (Chapter 02 — Transport §1.4).

clientIdTemplate.description: MQTT 5.0 Client ID template. The station substitutes {stationId} with its own stationId before sending CONNECT. The resulting Client ID MUST equal the CN in the station's TLS client certificate (Chapter 02 — Transport §1.2 / Chapter 06 — Security §3.3).

topicPrefix.description: MQTT topic namespace prefix. The station composes its publish/subscribe topics as {topicPrefix}/stations/{stationId}/{to-server|to-station}. The v1 segment is a topic namespace, NOT the OSPP protocol version (Chapter 02 — Transport §2.2).

qosLevel.description: MQTT Quality of Service level for OSPP messages. Chapter 02 — Transport §3.1 mandates QoS 1 for ALL OSPP messages (at-least-once delivery, defense for billing-critical traffic). QoS 0 MUST NOT be used; QoS 2 is not currently used by the protocol.

keepAliveSeconds.description: MQTT 5.0 Keep Alive interval the station MUST use on CONNECT. Chapter 02 — Transport §1.2 specifies 30 seconds (broker disconnects after 45s of silence using a 1.5x multiplier).

cleanStart.description: MQTT 5.0 Clean Start flag. Chapter 02 — Transport §1.2 mandates false so persistent sessions retain queued messages across brief disconnects. This is REQUIRED for the buffered-offline-message guarantees described in Chapter 01 — Architecture §6.5.

sessionExpirySeconds.description: MQTT 5.0 Session Expiry Interval (seconds). Chapter 02 — Transport §1.2 specifies 3600 (1 hour) — broker retains undelivered QoS 1 messages and subscriptions for this duration after a station disconnect.

tlsVersion.description: Minimum TLS version. Chapter 06 — Security §4.1 / Chapter 02 — Transport §1.3 mandate TLS 1.3 only. TLS 1.2 fallback MUST NOT be permitted; TLS 0-RTT MUST NOT be enabled.

mqttVersion.description: MQTT protocol version the broker speaks. Chapter 02 — Transport §1.1 mandates MQTT 5.0; MQTT 3.1.1 is NOT supported.

lastWillTopic.description: OPTIONAL Last Will and Testament topic template. When present the station SHOULD publish a ConnectionLost LWT (MSG-011) on this topic at MQTT CONNECT. The {stationId} placeholder, if present, is substituted by the station. Defaults to {topicPrefix}/stations/{stationId}/to-server when omitted (Chapter 02 — Transport §4.3).

mqttConfig.description: MQTT 5.0 broker connection parameters. Defaults align with Chapter 02 — Transport §1.2 normative values; servers SHOULD NOT advertise values that contradict §1.2 (the schema rejects QoS≠1, cleanStart≠false, mqttVersion≠5.0, tlsVersion≠1.3 to enforce this).
